Un Viajes al Corazon del Cusco Colonial

Bienvenidos a Terra Andina Colonial Mansion, donde la elegancia de la piedra y la madera del siglo XVIII se encuentran con el lujo contemporáneo. Ubicados a solo unos pasos del histórico Mercado de San Pedro, ofrecemos una experiencia única de inmersión en el arte y la historia cusqueña.

Terra Andina Colonial Mansion

The Facade of Terra Andina Colonial Mansion stands as a magnificent example of Cusco’s viceregal architecture. The manor features solid stone walls beneath viceregal walls and lanterns; and traditional balconies, strategically located just half a block from the San Pedro Central Market, which boasts an iconic iron structure designed by Gustave Eiffel. The main entrance, guarded by an imposing wooden gate adorned with gilded bronze medallions, sits have a block from the historic San Pedro Church, creating an unparalleled architectural setting.

Welcome Desk

Welcome Desk Our reception welcomes you amidst Inca stone walls, exquisite wood carvings, and paintings using the same techniques as the Cusco School, all framed and gleaming like gold leaf; a first encounter with colonial history and art.

Welcome Desk

Welcome Desk at Terra Andina Colonial Mansion is a space that seamlessly blends Cusco's living history, where imposing Inca stone walls serve as the foundation for exquisite colonial decor. The welcome area features intricate carved woodwork and a stunning collection similar to “Cusco School” paintings, whose religious motifs and ornate like gold-leaf frames create a warm, solemn 'Museum-Mansion' atmosphere.

The Colonial Patio - Lobby

The elegance of colonial stone and wood converges in the front view. Majestic stone silhouettes guard the whispers of the 1700s, where time seems to have stood still. Monuments that capture the essence of Colonial Art. Those colonial arches framed the landscape with the nostalgia of a golden era. Beneath the porticos, the sunset light played hide-and-seek among the worn stone columns.

Panorama from the left flank of the Lobby, at the majestic Terra Andina Colonial Mansion. An architectural canvas that unfolds the whisper of Andean History, captured from the colonial arches of the mansion.

The Colonial Patio - Lobby

Our lobby is graced by majestic colonial arches dating back to the 1700s, framed by expansive hand-carved wooden windows that pay tribute to the legacy of the artisans from our ancestral land."

The Lobby or Colonial Patio was the Hotel’s Restaurant prior to he expansion in 2024.

For larger groups, the Colonial Patio can be utilized as an additional restaurant space, with a total capacity of up to 108 people

Small Patio

Small Patio before the restaurant. Starting your day in this hall is like diving into the artistic soul of Peru. The walls showcase a collection of themes from the Cusco School of Painting, featuring delicate canvases set within stunning gold-leaf frames. Between the celestial glow of the gilded frames and the colonial motifs, the buffet offers a feast of flavors in a setting where the morning light makes the art shimmer, turning breakfast into an unforgettable aesthetic experience.

The Breakfast Buffet

The Breakfast Buffet area at Terra Andina Colonial Mansion, In Terra restaurant, preserves the historical essence and is distinguished by its magnificent colonials from the 1700s. Built using the ancient dry stone method, these romantic columns frame an inner saloon that fills the space with warm light. The atmosphere is enhanced by travertine tables, elegant wrought-iron chairs, and walls display canvases that are like those of the "Cuzco School" and their characteristic frames resemble gold leaf, creating an artistic and stately continuity throughout the main level. The walls display canvases that are like those of the "Cuzco School" and their characteristic frames resemble gold leaf, creating an artistic and stately continuity throughout the main level.

In-Terra Restaurant

In-Terra Restaurant at Terra Andina Mansión Colonial. Capacity: 50 people using long tables, or 68 people using individual tables. Lo

Bar

This bar is a journey through time where two worlds converge. To the right, the imposing solidity of stone walls pays tribute to pre-Hispanic legacy, while the colonial arches tell the story of Spanish influence. Facing this ancient heritage, sophistication takes center stage with mirrors framed in imitation gold leaf, creating an opulent atmosphere that balances raw history with contemporary luxury.

Elysium Spa

This spa is a sanctuary of serenity hidden within the walls of a majestic colonial mansion. Modern design seamlessly intertwines with the original architecture, where state-of-the-art wellness technology coexists with the “Spanish Colonial era”. Featuring intelligent hydrotherapy circuits, minimalist treatment suites, and elite personalized service, every corner is crafted to transform well-being into an unforgettable experience that honors the past while embracing the future of relaxation.

Master King Suite

Our Master King Suite redefines luxury with a spacious King bed and independent living areas.Designed for ultimate privacy, it features premium finishes and breathtaking views that invite absolute relaxation The exquisite hand-carved woodwork, crafted with unparalleled artistry, blends seamlessly with contemporary luxury to create a space fit for royalty. Staying in the Master Suite is like living inside a work of art—a retreat where time stands still and the grandeur of the past embraces the most exclusive modern comforts. Detalles de Ultra-Lujo / Ultra-Luxury Details:Arte Sacro: Pinturas originales de la Escuela Cusqueña con marcos en pan de oro.Arquitectura: Arcos de piedra originales y carpintería artística tallada a mano.Ambiente: El espacio más amplio y privilegiado de la casona colonial.Experiencia: Diseñada para los viajeros más exigentes que buscan una inmersión histórica total.

Master Suite

From this vantage point, the Master Suite reveals its finest feature: a dream closet functional yet contemporary in its design.

Master King Bathroom

Junior Suite King

Experience the height of comfort in our Junior Suite Kings , which offers a spacious layout with a plush King bed and an inviting living area.

Premium Room

with two Queen Size Beds Exclusivity in the Heart of the Mansion. Our Premium Rooms are the crown jewels of our 18th-century colonial estate. Strategically located around the majestic courtyard of the new extension, these rooms are an ode to modern art, standing out because they frame the featuring elegant and exquisite design that defines the space. As part of a limited and exclusive collection, they offer an unparalleled atmosphere of privacy and prestige. Here, the whispers of history meet modern luxury, allowing you to wake up surrounded by Cusco’s most authentic architecture in a setting that perfectly captures the soul of the 1700s.

Classic Room

with Two Twin Size Bed. A Sanctuary of History and Comfort Step back into the 18th century in our Classic Rooms, where Cusco’s colonial architecture meets contemporary elegance. Spanning these spaces are meticulously designed to provide a restful sanctuary after exploring the Imperial City. Each room features refined finishes and furnishings that evoke the grandeur of the past, seamlessly paired with modern essentials like a Smart TV and high-speed Wi-Fi. Relax in the warmth of independent heating and the softness of premium linens, ensuring a stay that is as cozy as it is unforgettable in the heart of historic Cusco.
